creator: vedran@miletic.net
text:
(In reply to Adam Weinberger from comment #1)
I only use packages from the official "latest" repository, and everything is up to date.

For the record, neovim works fine, it's just that pkg gives this error which I find quite unusual given that lpeg.so is found by ldd:

# ldd /usr/local/bin/nvim
/usr/local/bin/nvim:
libluv.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libluv.so.1 (0x1e590fae8000)
libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x1e5911a24000)
/usr/local/lib/lua/5.1/lpeg.so (0x1e590fb2b000)
libintl.so.8 => /usr/local/lib/libintl.so.8 (0x1e59105c9000)
libm.so.5 => /lib/libm.so.5 (0x1e59109a8000)
libutil.so.9 => /lib/libutil.so.9 (0x1e591189f000)
libdl.so.1 => /usr/lib/libdl.so.1 (0x1e5913406000)
libkvm.so.7 => /lib/libkvm.so.7 (0x1e59123d7000)
librt.so.1 => /lib/librt.so.1 (0x1e5912f76000)
libthr.so.3 => /lib/libthr.so.3 (0x1e59143fc000)
libgcc_s.so.1 => /lib/libgcc_s.so.1 (0x1e5914a45000)
libuv.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libuv.so.1 (0x1e5915033000)
libelf.so.2 => /lib/libelf.so.2 (0x1e5915e9c000)
[vdso] (0x1e590f147000)

Could this be an issue in pkg?

I tried

# pkg upgrade -fg 'lua*'

but there was no change in pkg check -Bdrs output.

I can also try reinstalling all packages if you think it would be worth it given this information.
